title: The Passport (Seconde Amendment) Rules, 1970 (28.08.1970)
text: REGISTERED No. . 221 The Gazette of India EXTRAORDINARY PART II—Section 3—Sub section (i) PUBLISHED BY AUTHORITY No. 156] NEW DELHI, SATURDAY, AUGUST 29,1970/BHADRA 7,1892 Separate paging is given to this Part in order that it may be filed as a separate compilation. MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th August 1970 G.S.R. 1267.~In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 24 of the Pass- ports Act, 1967 (15 of 1967), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further to amend the Passports Rules, 1967, namely:— 1. (1) These rules may be called the Passports (Second Amendment) Rules, 1970. (2) They shall come Into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In Schedule II of the Passports Rules, 1967, under the heading "PART I— PASSPORTS", in the Table, for item III and the entries relating thereto the following item and the entries relating thereto shall be substituted, namely: — "III. Diplomatic Passports (1) (a) Officers of the Indian Foreign Service (Branch A). (b) Such officers of the Indian Foreign Service (Branch B) and other officers of the Ministry of External Affairs and other Ministries or Depart- ments of the Government of India who are proceeding abroad on ( 707 ) -- 1 of 4 -- 7o8 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PART I n - official business or are posted to Indian Missions or Postg abroad, as may be determined by the Central Government. (c) The following members of the family of an official referred to in (a) and (b) above, when travelling with, or joining, such officer at his post abroad, namely:— Wife or official hostess, husband, dependent sons under 24 years and dependent unmarried daughter's, dependent father and mother. NOTE.—Any member of the family referred to in (c) above of an cT-iccr who is actually holding a diplomatic assignment abroarl tr-.ay also issued a diplomatic passport for staying in a countrv other than the country of accreditation of the officer for study or ether approved purposes; a diplomatic passport Issued in such a case shall, however, be surrendered when the diplomatic assignment of th» officer ig terminated or when the officer is posted bade to the headquarters. (2) (a) A person having or having been granted a diplomatic status, either because of the nature of his foreign mission or because of the position he holds, as may be determined by the Central Government. (b) Wife or husband, dependent sons under 24 years and dependent un- married daughters of a person referred to in (a) above when accom- panying the said person." [No. VII/405/17/69.] S. K.
